segments.text: By the rivers of Babylon we sat and wept when we remembered Zion.
There on the poplars we hung our harps,
for there our captors asked us for songs, our tormentors demanded songs of joy;
Sing us one of the songs of Zion!
segments.speaker: Captors

segments.text: How can we sing the songs of the Lord while in a foreign land?
segments.speaker: Israelite Exiles

segments.text: If I forget you, Jerusalem, may my right hand forget its skill.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: May my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth if I do not remember you, if I do not consider Jerusalem my highest joy.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Remember, Lord, what the Edomites did on the day Jerusalem fell.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Tear it down, tear it down to its foundations!
segments.speaker: Edomites

segments.text: Happy is the one who repays you according to what you have done to us.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Happy is the one who seizes your infants and dashes them against the rocks.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
